model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: Miami (FL) holds a clear analytical edge with superior KenPom (30 vs 52) and Torvik (35 vs 46) rankings, along with a stronger 25-8 record compared to Missouri's 20-12. The Hurricanes' balanced efficiency on both ends of the floor (offensive rank 34-35, defensive rank 45-68) should exploit Missouri's weaker defensive profile. While Missouri's Caleb Grill appears available after his earlier injury scare, Miami's overall talent depth and experience in the competitive ACC give them the advantage in this 7/10 matchup.

reasoningStep.title: Miami (FL) vs Missouri Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Miami (FL) is favored based on superior metrics and record, though Missouri has some offensive firepower.

evidence.injuryContext: Missouri's leading scorer Caleb Grill suffered a head/neck injury in late November 2024 but appears available for the tournament based on recent articles mentioning his participation.
evidence.matchupFactors: Miami's defensive efficiency (rank 45-68) should challenge Missouri's offense, while Miami's balanced attack can exploit Missouri's weaker defense (rank 68-104).
evidence.historicalContext: Both teams from power conferences (ACC vs SEC), but Miami has better overall metrics and fewer losses.
